scenario: Sable Bank stores an opaque text reference such as the value valid on every booking entry for 7600 transactions per day in Camunda 8.8. A FEEL expression on an input mapping must build a String literal that can span multiple lines and include escaped characters such as a backslash or a newline character.
question: How does the FEEL data types reference describe the syntax for a String literal value?

options.text: It uses a sequence of characters enclosed in single quotes, supports only the escaped backslash and forbids any whitespace character such as a newline inside the literal value
It uses a sequence of characters enclosed in backticks, supports escaped quotes only and treats any unrecognized escape sequence inside the literal as a fatal evaluation error

It uses a sequence of characters enclosed in double quotes that may contain escaped characters such as a backslash, quote, newline, tab and Unicode escape, and the literal can span multiple lines as documented
It uses a sequence of characters enclosed in angle brackets, supports only the Unicode escape form for special characters and disallows any multi-line content inside the literal

a.text: Incorrect. The documented String literal uses double quotes rather than single quotes and supports a broader escape set than the single backslash.

b.text: Incorrect. Backticks are not the documented delimiter for a FEEL String literal, and the escape set covers more than only escaped quotes.

c.text: Correct. The String entry of the data types reference documents a sequence of characters enclosed in double quotes that can contain escaped characters such as backslash, single and double quote, newline, carriage return, tab and Unicode escapes, and the literal can span multiple lines.

d.text: Incorrect. Angle brackets are not the documented delimiter for a FEEL String literal, and the documented literal supports multi-line content.

explanation: A FEEL String is a sequence of characters enclosed in double quotes that may contain escaped characters such as backslash, quote, newline, carriage return, tab and Unicode escapes, and the literal can span multiple lines.
